Combine this with the ‘real cumulative delta” indicator will have you winning every trade. It’s an order flow indicator that basically tells the future based on what sellers and buyers are actually doing. Also known CVD(cumulative volume delta)

To all the beginners who are going to start trading , I want to tell you guys that I have been trading in Crypto for the last 2 years and i want to give you some rules that you should follow which most of the traders don’t :-

Rule 1 :- Don’t Overtrade. Limit yourself for taking only 2 trades per day. If the market is moving as per your strategy then take 3 trades , Not more than that.

Rule 2 :- Protect your Capital first. Risk Management is the most important thing in trading which most of the people ignore. Before making profits and losses , you should protect your capital first.

Rule 3 :- Not taking trades is also Trading. If the market is not moving as per your strategy , Don’t trade , Just Observe tha market for that day.

Rule 4 :- Consistency and Discipline. Trust me if you master these 2 things , the above 3 rules will get easier.

Rule 5 :- Accept the losses. Losses are a part of trading and the best thing you can do is to learn the mistakes that led you to that loss.

Rule 6 :- Be patient. Trading is not a job where you will get consistent return like salary , someday you will make huge profit and someday you will make huge losses. So , Be Patient.

And TRUST YOUR STRATEGY. Use strategies which involve SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE , EMA , VWAP etc. 

And the last thing that i want to tell you guys is that don’t use so many indicators.

INDICATORS ARE BASED ON HOW THE MARKET MOVES , MARKET IS NOT BASED ON HOW THE INDICATOR MOVES.

I hope you understand this line.

When hedge fund traders themselves say they use VWAP, You know this is trading right along with where traders will want to get the best value for the price. 

A vwap tells so much more than where the price is, it tells you when the price is at steep premium or steep discount and it always finds it way back to the balanced price (Vwap line). 

In layman's terms, VWAP is like the original manufacturer's price of a product as it leaves the factory. 

When price is above VWAP, people are buying/selling it at a premium

When price is below the VWAP, people are buying/selling it at a discount.

Price will always gravitate back to its balance point, the  "Fair value price" 

-

On the charts, i use a combination of Monthly VWAP and mapping of swing highs and swing lows to see where price is sitting and if the monthly VWAP is sitting too unrealistically far to day-trade with, I will scale down to the weekly VWAP. 

Session VWAPs are fine if you are looking for scalps. 

My confluence is that i must see price pivoting around and hugging the VWAP to ascertain if its a trade or no trade day. 

-

Retest of VWAP - ideally a rejection or slice through of the vwap are the only 2 things that happens - Rejection or Slice Through. 

When price bounces off the VWAP, i'll wait for a few M15 candle bodies to close to confirm the rejection and possibly enter longs or shorts targeting the next logical liquidity pool. 

If price slices right through the VWAP, it is either a no trade or i look for confluence to enter - Sweep of the next logical liquidity pool or point of interest and look for the retracement.

The best combination i have found that works for me on Monthly/Weekly VWAP is the take out of a High Pivot or Low Pivot and a retracement back to the Vwap.  

-

It is simple, clean - does not work ALL the time, but it gives a very good idea of where price is and where price should be.

Hello sir, I have to say this was very clear and understandable. Sometimes, people try to explain things and get way too technical. I've tried to use different strategies that just don't work. I know you can't get rich over night or in a few days. But I would like to be able to trade and make some profit. Thanks brother for making this strategy. Hopefully I can master this.

Backtested it on FX Replay.

Time period - 1st January to 23rd September, 2025

Pair - XAU/USD
Captial - 100,000
End Captial - 2.13 mil (risking only 1% per trade) (the risk increases as the captial grows)

Total trades - 344
Wins - 210
Losers - 134
Win rate - 61.05%
RR - 1.5 to 2 (on good days even 7)
Best session - Post London open to pre New york
Best days - Monday 61%, Tuesday 63%, Thursday 72%
Worst days - Wednesday 54%, Friday 55%

I would say it’s a solid strategy for a consistent capital growth.
